title: Strawberry Flavored
description: The coziest, most feature-rich, and astonishingly optimized performance & QOL modpack — by Rift Studios.
body:
![Strawberry Flavored Banner](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4f3bb6067729f59caf824a8c37e40cc784f26191.png)

# 🍓 Welcome to Strawberry Flavored!
**The performance and QOL modpack with _nothing_ left out.**

Strawberry Flavored features: Astonishingly increased FPS, _hundreds_ of quality of life improvements, helpful tools for general play OR complex creation, full Optifine feature support with Sodium, tons of visual enhancements, and so much more!

Strawberry Flavored is constantly updating to the most recent versions of Minecraft. Feel free to use an old version of Strawberry Flavored, just be aware of potential issues when doing so. **We will not provide support for outdated versions of Strawberry Flavored.**

# ❓ FAQ/Questions

Q. **Why choose this over another performance modpack?**

A. Most other performance modpacks have only a select few performance and quality of life mods. Some performance modpacks have QOL mods that another doesnt. This pack strives to have it all, though. Additionally, most popular performance modpacks have become very professional, commercial, and full of advertisements, while we try to maintain a sense of community and chill vibes.

Q. **What kind of QOL features can I expect?**

A. Tons. Improvements to chat and GUI, the best sound improvements you can find, _FULL_ Optifine feature support with Sodium, improvements for building, improvements to the vanilla visual experience with new particles and weather, built in optional voice chat, and SO SO MUCH MORE!

Q. **Can I add my own mods/what's compatible?**

A. The best way to know is just to check. If you have further questions, feel free to join our Discord server and ask!

## 📜 We Recommend Allocating 8GB RAM to Strawberry Flavored

If you have enough RAM, we recommend increasing it on your modpack in order to have a better, smoother experience. To do so, go to the modpack settings -> Java and memory -> check "Custom memory allocation" -> set it to 8192 (or around!) and you're good to go! :)
